description:
About Us

We prioritize hiring individuals based on their core values rather than solely focusing on hard skills. Our approach is to empower exceptional candidates to excel in a role by leveraging their strengths, regardless of whether they precisely match a job description. As a result, we welcome applicants with varying levels of experience, both exceeding and falling short of the requirements outlined below.

PixelPiew is a privately owned studio specializing in design, branding, and creative services. Our mission is to drive the evolution and prosperity of financial services, B2B, corporate, and non-profit organizations. We achieve this by harnessing the power of research and craftsmanship to create refined brand identities, websites, messaging, campaigns, and marketing materials.

We Want

We Are Actively Seeking Skilled And Enthusiastic Freelance Designers To Join Our Roster Of Creative Professionals. This Is a Broad Application, And We Invite All Designers To Apply, With a Special Emphasis On Those Who Excel In The Following Areas:

  • Logo and Brand Design
  • Motion Graphics Design
  • UX Design
  • Print Collateral Design

Please be aware that while we may not be able to respond to every applicant immediately, we will retain all submissions for potential future opportunities. If you are passionate about design and are eager to tackle exciting and demanding projects, we sincerely encourage you to reach out to us!

Compensation

This is a freelance, remote position, but you are also welcome to work on-site at our Providence, RI location if you prefer. We offer competitive compensation that aligns with your level of expertise and experience.

title: Data Quality Analyst
description:
Weâre building applied AI systems for highâstakes, realâworld decisions. Our platform ingests and reasons over large, messy data to surface evidence with hard constraints around fairness, auditability, and low bias.

The single gnarliest, most impactâheavy place this problem shows up is in insurance claims, so thatâs where weâre deployed today, running in production at some of the largest carriers in North America.

As a Data Quality Analyst, you will play a pivotal role in leveraging data to ensure the integrity and accuracy of insurance claims processing. Pulling from open data sources, you will support our clients in monitoring, detecting, and preventing potential illegitimate claims.

This is a remote, part-time role (15-30 hours/week).

Responsibilities & Scope:

  • Conduct investigations utilizing open data sources and in partnership with Owlâs AI.
  • Ensure findings are inputted logically, leveraging our internal platform to guide the process.
  • Analyze external claimant data for quality, completeness, and accuracy.
  • Efficiently utilize time and resources to deliver high-quality results that maximize value for the company.
  • Maintain the security and confidentiality of sensitive information.
  • Operate within company security and disclosure guidelines.

Requirements:

  • Must be available to work at least 15 hours/week.
  • Previous experience in open-source content research.
  • Ability to work independently in a remote environment.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills for documenting research findings effectively.
  • Very comfortable on the computer - ability to type quickly, utilize various search functions and keyboard shortcuts.
  • Analytical thinker with the ability to exercise creative problem-solving during investigations.
  • Ability to adapt quickly to evolving technologies and system processes.
  • Due to the remote nature of this role, secure & reliable internet connection is critical to be considered.

Benefits:

The compensation for this role is $20.00 per hour.

  • Bring your friends on board! Help grow our team of top performers and receive a cash bonus through our uncapped, generous Employee Referral Program.

title: Designer Residential
description:
NORR is a global team of 800 architects, engineers, planners, and interior designers creating design strategies and solutions that express the unique vision of every project. Driven by a common purpose with our clients and partners, we share ideas to create and innovate, bringing people, design, and technologies together to think bigger and act bolder.

Our story began in 1938 and has evolved into an employee-owned, fully integrated firm committed to sustainable development goals across 13 market sectors in Canada, the US, and UK. Design excellence continues to guide everything we do.

At NORR, our residential portfolio spans decades and project types. Our approach to multi-family residential design supports smart growth and walkable neighborhoods with building wellness initiatives, integrated carbon-reduction strategies, innovative construction technologies, and resilient infrastructure. Every solution is guided by principles of design excellence, urban regeneration, inclusive context, and marketability.

Learn more about our Residential Portfolio.

As a Designer, you will work with other members of the design team in order to develop design solutions appropriate to the context, budget and aesthetic requirements of architectural projects. You will collaborate with other professional disciplines and have opportunities to coordinate the design through all phases of the project.

Duties and Responsibilities

  • Compiles information from reports and published documents; evaluates and analyzes the information to understand broad requirements of project
  • Develops architectural designs, to ensure an exemplary solution is delivered that meets quality parameters, project timelines and budgetary requirements
  • Coordinates design intent with other disciplines
  • Integrates engineering requirements, site surveys and field review information, adhering to applicable codes and jurisdictional requirements
  • Prepares design reports, drawings and models following office standards
  • Performs site visitations to determine compliance with construction documents; identifies any deviations and liaises with owner and contactor to resolve issues in a timely manner
  • Reviews project products, services, and shop drawings to ensure adherence to project documents, relevant jurisdictional codes and corporate quality controls

Required Skills

  • Experience in all phases of the project cycle is required
  • Architectural design software skills including Revit, AutoCAD, SketchUp, PhotoShop, and other architectural design software
  • Familiarity with MEP and Structural building systems and building codes is required
  • Have successfully completed related post-secondary education
  • Registered with a relevant professional architectural association is an asset

Required Experience

  • 7-10 years of project work experience
  • Experience in Residential projects is an asset
  • Salary Range: $70,000.00 - $105,000.00 /annually

title: Customer Experience Specialist
description:
At DraftKings, AI is becoming an integral part of both our present and future, powering how work gets done today, guiding smarter decisions, and sparking bold ideas. Itâs transforming how we enhance customer experiences, streamline operations, and unlock new possibilities. Our teams are energized by innovation and readily embrace emerging technology. Weâre not waiting for the future to arrive. Weâre shaping it, one bold step at a time. To those who see AI as a driver of progress, come build the future together.

The Crown Is Yours

As a Customer Experience Specialist, youâll help deliver exceptional customer experiences by partnering closely with our vendors and internal teams. Youâll support the day-to-day success of our outsourced operations, helping ensure customers receive consistent, high-quality support across every interaction. Working across Customer Experience, Shared Services, Analytics, Risk, and Technology teams, youâll solve operational challenges, identify opportunities for improvement, and drive performance that enhances the customer experience.

What you'll do

  • Support the operational performance of Customer Experience vendors, helping ensure service level objectives, quality standards, and customer satisfaction goals are consistently achieved.
  • Serve as a key partner to vendor teams by resolving operational questions, coordinating cross-functional support, and helping remove obstacles that impact performance and the customer experience.
  • Review customer interactions, operational trends, and performance metrics to identify improvement opportunities, provide actionable feedback, and support continuous improvement initiatives.
  • Facilitate calibration sessions, performance reviews, associate huddles, and operational communications to reinforce quality expectations, share updates, and drive alignment across teams.
  • Support operational escalations and customer-impacting incidents by coordinating responses, monitoring emerging risks and performance trends, and partnering with stakeholders to drive timely resolution.


What you'll bring

  • A Bachelorâs Degree in Business, Communications, Operations, or a related field, or equivalent combination of education and professional experience.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to build effective relationships across internal teams and external partners.
  • Excellent analytical, critical thinking, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.
  • The ability to influence stakeholders and collaborate effectively in a matrixed environment without direct authority.
  • Experience managing multiple priorities while maintaining strong attention to detail in a fast-paced and evolving environment.
  • Proficiency with Microsoft Office applications, Google Workspace tools, and other business productivity platforms.
  • Flexibility and adaptability to navigate changing business priorities and operational needs.
  • A customer-first mindset with a passion for improving experiences, driving continuous improvement, and raising the bar for service excellence.


Join Our Team

Weâre a publicly traded (NASDAQ: DKNG) technology company headquartered in Boston. As a regulated gaming company, you may be required to obtain a gaming license issued by the appropriate state agency as a condition of employment. Donât worry, weâll guide you through the process if this is relevant to your role.

title: Insta
description:
"2NS is a domestic steadily growing information security company. At the center of our operations are a passion for information security, customer satisfaction and the satisfaction of our own employees, as well as their opportunity to develop.

Our range of services is extensive and our staff are experts in the field of information security. We implement a wide variety of projects for our customers in the areas of technical and administrative information security. Our customer profile is broad and we have customers from many different fields. We operate globally and we have customers in Finland and, for example, other Nordic countries.

It is also important for us to support the development of information security and the diversity of the industry, which is why we act as sponsors in several different hacker events and communities such as Disobey, HelSec and Women4Cyber Finland."

title: Rippling
description:
The verdict

Rippling is one of the strongest products you can buy if you run HR, IT, and finance as connected systems, and the review data backs that up: 4.8 across more than 14,000 G2 reviews and 4.9 on Capterra. The onboarding automation that provisions payroll, benefits, IT access, and a device in one move is the standout, and it earns its keep. Go in clear-eyed about two things: the modular, quote-based pricing stacks faster than people expect, and the first-week learning curve is real.

Key features

One employee record that drives everything

Hire someone once and the record propagates everywhere: payroll, benefits, device provisioning, app access, and offboarding all read from it, so a department change updates downstream automatically.

Automated payroll and tax compliance

Rippling calculates payroll taxes and files them with the correct federal, state, and local agencies, supports multi-entity and global payroll for employees and contractors, and stores electronic W-2s and 1099s.

One-click onboarding and offboarding

A single flow sends offer letters, enrolls payroll and benefits, ships a device, and grants app logins; offboarding runs it in reverse, revoking access and triggering device returns.

Benefits administration with payroll sync

Over 500 EDI and API carrier integrations, integrated HSA/FSA/commuter accounts with automatic payroll-deduction sync, COBRA and ACA automation, and bring-your-own-broker support alongside Rippling's PEO and brokerage.

600+ out-of-the-box integrations

Connects QuickBooks, Xero, NetSuite, Guideline for 401(k), Checkr for background checks, plus identity and productivity tools, with real user provisioning, attribute mapping, and cross-app workflow automation.

Modular HR, IT, and Finance products

Buy the core Rippling Platform, then add only the products you need across HR (Payroll, Benefits, Learning, Performance), IT (Identity, Device, Inventory), and Finance (Corporate Cards, Expenses, Bill Pay).

What it is

Rippling is an HR platform that doesnât stop at HR. The core is a single employee record: hire someone once, and that record drives payroll, benefits, device provisioning, app access, and offboarding. Where most HR software treats the employee database as a system of record, Rippling treats it as a trigger. Change a personâs department and Rippling updates it everywhere downstream. Onboard a new hire and it enrolls them in payroll, sets up their benefits, ships a laptop, and grants the right app logins from the same flow.

The product is sold in modules. Thereâs a core, required Rippling Platform, and then you buy what you need on top of it: HR products (Payroll, Benefits, Learning, Performance, Recruiting, Time & Attendance), IT products (Identity & Access, Device, Inventory), and Finance products (Corporate Cards, Expenses, Bill Pay). The pitch, and itâs a real one, is that all three sit on the same data, so the IT and finance sides arenât bolted-on afterthoughts.

Who itâs for

This is built for the company that has outgrown a point HR tool but is also tired of running payroll in one place, device management in another, and expense reports in a third. If youâre hiring across multiple states or countries, Ripplingâs strongest. It runs multi-entity and global payroll, stores W-2s and 1099s, and handles contractors through dedicated products. Reviewers who pay people internationally describe currency conversion and cross-border pay as something Rippling âjust takes care of.â

Itâs a worse fit for a simple operation. If you have fifteen domestic employees, one benefits plan, and no real IT footprint, the all-in-one model is overkill, and reviewers say exactly that, warning that simpler teams risk overbuying. Youâd be paying for breadth you wonât use and absorbing a configuration burden you donât need. Rippling rewards companies that genuinely run HR, IT, and finance as connected systems. If those live in separate departments that never talk, you lose the main reason to buy it.

Why it stands out

Onboarding and offboarding is the standout, and itâs not close. Capterra logs 97% positive mentions on onboarding, with reviewers describing clear step-by-step guidance to add employees, configure payroll, and connect benefits. business.com calls it a major strength. The thing people keep describing is âone-click onboardingâ that provisions payroll, IT access, and a device in a single action, and the same automation runs in reverse at offboarding, revoking access and triggering device returns. The January 2026 release added advanced Microsoft 365 offboarding actions, which is exactly the kind of detail that matters when someone leaves.

The unified platform is the second genuine win. Capterra users rate the all-in-one dashboard 98% positive, and reviewers repeatedly call out Ripplingâs UI as the best theyâve used in an HRMS. Collapsing payroll, benefits, and people data into one synced record removes the manual re-entry and the reconciliation errors that plague stitched-together stacks.

Integration breadth is the third. Rippling ships 600+ (the FAQ says 650+) out-of-the-box integrations: QuickBooks, Xero, NetSuite, Guideline for 401(k), Checkr for background checks, plus the usual identity and productivity tools, with real provisioning and attribute mapping, not just data export. Reviewers call this exceptional. The one caveat worth flagging: Expert Market notes Rippling doesnât natively integrate with third-party HR systems, so the strength is in app provisioning and internal cross-module sync rather than swapping out parts of the HR stack.

Pricing in plain language

Rippling starts around $8 per employee per month, but that number tells you very little. The real model is modular and quote-based: thereâs no full price list on the site, and some products carry a monthly base fee on top of the per-employee rate. You buy the core platform, then add each product separately. Thatâs the flexibility selling point and the budgeting headache in one.

Hereâs where the wall is: the modules stack. Independent reviewers are consistent on this, the per-employee base is reasonable, but as you add Benefits, Device Management, Expenses, and the rest, the total climbs in ways that arenât obvious upfront. The most common complaint across G2, Capterra, and third-party reviews isnât that Rippling is bad value, itâs that the total cost is hard to see coming. Benefits administration is a particular gotcha: itâs a separate paid module, which is why business.com calls benefits a ânotable weaknessâ on cost grounds, though those fees can be waived if Rippling is your broker or PEO.

Get a real quote before you fall in love with the demo, and price the modules youâll actually turn on, not the ones that look nice in the sales deck.

Limitations

The learning curve is real. The same feature density that makes Rippling powerful makes it overwhelming on day one. G2 reviewers describe it as a lot to absorb, especially for new admins. This is the cost of the all-in-one model, and itâs worth budgeting setup time for.

Stability is the more concerning one. Capterra reviewers flag platform bugs and slow loads (48% negative) and auth or session timeouts (46% negative), and the mobile app comes up as occasionally buggy and missing features the desktop version has. More seriously, payroll, the thing you most need to be boring and correct, has edge cases. Capterra reviewers report incorrect overtime calculations that forced them to claw back overpayments, and Thrivea notes tax edge-case issues. The payroll engine is rated highly overall, with a 100% error-free filing guarantee cited by business.com, but the exceptions are the kind you remember.

Reporting is the quieter gap. 43% of Capterra mentions on reporting are negative, centered on a lack of customization. If you need to slice your people data in non-standard ways, expect to fight the report builder. Benefits enrollment is the last one worth naming: Thrivea reports the workflows can feel clunky or incomplete, which is part of why benefits sentiment runs mixed even though the payroll-to-deduction sync is well liked.

The bottom line

If youâre a growing company running HR, IT, and finance as connected systems, especially across states or borders, Rippling is one of the strongest products you can buy, and the review data backs that up. The onboarding automation alone earns its keep, and the unified record genuinely removes work. Buy it for the integration, not just the HR.

But go in clear-eyed. Get a real quote and add up every module youâll switch on, because the stacking is where people get surprised. Budget setup time for the learning curve. And if youâre a small, single-country team with simple benefits and no IT footprint, this is more platform than you need, youâll pay for breadth you wonât touch. Rippling rewards complexity. Bring it the right kind, and itâs excellent.

What people are saying online

Rippling is one of the most highly rated HR platforms I track, 4.8 across more than 14,000 G2 reviews and 4.9 on Capterra. Users love the unified dashboard and the way onboarding fires off payroll, benefits, and IT setup in one move. The platform is most positive for growing companies that genuinely run HR, IT, and finance together and have the bandwidth to configure it. The recurring frustrations are the same ones every time: a steep first-week learning curve, occasional bugs and slow loads, and a modular price tag that stacks up faster than people expect.

Drawn from independent reviews and discussions, separate from our methodology score.

What people love

  • Onboarding logs 97% positive mentions, one click provisions payroll, benefits, IT access, and ships the device
  • Capterra
  • All-in-one platform rated 98% positive for collapsing payroll, benefits, and people data into one synced record
  • Capterra
  • Cited as the best HRMS UI reviewers have used, intuitive and genuinely modern
  • Multiple
  • Direct deposit (97%) and payroll entry (95%) rated highly; international pay 'just gets taken care of'
  • G2
  • 600+ third-party app integrations praised as exceptional for provisioning and cross-app automation
  • Independent reviews

Common complaints

  • Sheer number of features overwhelms new users; admin configuration carries a real learning curve
  • G2
  • Platform stability complaints, bugs and slow loads (48% negative) plus auth/session timeouts (46% negative)
  • Capterra
  • Reporting lacks customization (43% negative)
  • Capterra
  • Payroll edge cases bite, including incorrect overtime calculations that forced overpayment recovery
  • Multiple
  • Opaque, quote-based pricing with add-ons that 'stack' into surprise total cost
  • Multiple

Rippling alternatives

#2

Gusto

3.9

#3

Deel

3.9

TriNet

3.6

Where Rippling ranks

  • HR Platforms #1 of 6 3.9

Best for ops-heavy and multi-country teams that want HR, IT, and finance running off one synced employee record.

title: Receptionist
description:
Job Title: Remote Therapist Receptionist

Location: Remote (EST)

Employment Type: Full-Time

About The Role

Evolve Psychiatry is seeking a compassionate, organized, and customer-service-oriented Remote Therapist Receptionist to support our growing team of licensed therapists. This individual serves as the primary administrative point of contact for therapy patients, ensuring a seamless experience from initial inquiry through ongoing treatment.

The ideal candidate is highly organized, empathetic, comfortable working in a fast-paced healthcare environment, and able to manage multiple priorities while maintaining exceptional patient care. This role requires excellent communication skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work independently in a fully remote environment.

Responsibilities:

  • Answer phone calls and route inquiries appropriately
  • Schedule, confirm and modify appointments using our EMR system
  • Verify insurance information and collect copays
  • Communicate efficiently with therapists, patients, and other staff
  • Handle sensitive information with discretion and confidentiality

Qualifications:

  • Previous experience in a medical receptionist role working directly with therapists preferred
  • Experience in psychiatry or behavioral health
  • Proficient with electronic medical records and general computer skills
  • Strong organizational abilities and multitasking skills within a remote environment
  • Professional appearance/demeanor
  • Experience working in a fastpaced administrative environment
  • Strong computer proficiency

Success in This Role:The Ideal candidate is someone who:

  • Enjoys helping patients and providing outstanding customer service.
  • Can confidently manage a high volume of phone calls and appointment scheduling.
  • Thrives in a fastpaced, remote environment.
  • Pays close attention to detail and follows processes consistently.
  • Communicates professionally with patients, therapists, and other team members.
  • Takes initiative and is comfortable solving problems independently.

Why Join Us?

  • Be a part of a respected and growing psychiatry practice
  • Work in a collaborative, missiondriven environment
  • Competitive pay and benefits package
  • Make a meaningful difference in patients' lives everday

title: Analyst
description:
Who are we?

At Leadership Connect, we believe that relationships are everything in business and in life. Founded by two former CEOs of S&P 500 companies and a former presidential press secretary, our premier information service is built for developing relationships that impact decisions in government, business, and media. Utilizing deep research expertise and cutting-edge technology, we help our clients win business and influence policy across various specialties and verticals. We are a close-knit team dedicated to helping each of our users make a difference.

Leadership Connect Canada (LCC), a subsidiary of Leadership Connect Inc., is our Canadian entity operating in the province of Quebec.

About The Role

Working within our Data Team, we are seeking an Analyst to conduct and perform research and analysis tasks to develop and maintain our core data assets, people intelligence. The incumbent will actively participate in the development of our data operations activities while ensuring the accuracy and quality of our people intelligence data product. This role is ideal for data enthusiasts and problem solvers, keen on exercising ownership with a passion for empowering users and customers with information solutions that drive sales enablement and improve the collaboration between our customers and the people who are mission-critical to their organization. We embrace a mindset of continuous learning, and if youâre passionate about data collection, analysis, and development, we want to hear from you!

Core Functions Of The Role

  • Execute comprehensive data collection, development, and analysis supporting operational processes with a focus on relevance, precision, and accuracy
  • Undertaking in-depth analysis into key entities within our People Intelligence data product ensuring current and accurate data
  • Employ data analysis techniques to discern patterns, derive insights, and identify data quality discrepancies.
  • Collaborate with interdisciplinary teams to appraise data quality or to develop workflows contributing to enhancements in data completeness
  • Perform in an agile environment, in an operational context while facilitating subject matter interactions between interdisciplinary teams

About You

  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience with an interest in people intelligence
  • 1-2 years of experience in data analysis and statistical research
  • Strong analytical skills and attention to detail
  • Strong organizational and project management skills
  • Strong time management practices and a proven ability to meet deadlines
  • Ability to communicate across teams
  • Proficiency in conducting sophisticated web-based research
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel or other data management systems
  • Practical knowledge of data analytics tools like PowerBI, QuickSight or others is a plus

$40,000 - $55,000 a year

To attract and engage high-level professionals, it is crucial to showcase our expertise, experience, and commitment to fostering valuable connections. We understand the importance of transparency when it comes to salary matters. Our research and analysis of the Quebec market serve as the foundation for determining appropriate salary ranges. However, we also recognize that factors like location, experience, skills alignment, and other compensation considerations may influence these ranges. At Leadership Connect, we place a strong emphasis on open communication and adaptability to ensure fair and competitive compensation. We invite talented individuals to join our team and help us create a workforce that is motivated and empowered to achieve new levels of success.

What Is It Like to Work Here?

We foster an environment that encourages all our team members to excel by offering competitive compensation for top talent. We believe in creating a friendly and enjoyable workplace, with regular team-building events, happy hours, and more. Transparency is key within our company, and we hold monthly town halls led by our CEO to address questions regarding business plans, product direction, and company goals. In addition, we provide flexible PTO policies to ensure you can enjoy your time outside the office and focus on your personal life.

Are you ready to join our dynamic team and build meaningful relationships that make a difference? Apply today!

Benefits/Rewards

  • Awesome Extended Health Care Plan
  • Dental Care
  • Life & Disability insurance
  • Health spending accounts
  • Unlimited Vacation Leave
  • 12 Paid Holidays
  • $3,000 Employee Referral Program
  • Employer contribution to VRSP
  • Rewards and recognition programs

If youâre excited about this role but donât meet 100% of the requirements, we still want to hear from you!

Leadership Connect is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce where unique experiences are valued and everyone has the opportunity to contribute. Research has continuously shown that women and people of color are less likely to apply for jobs unless they meet all of the listed qualifications. We want to help overcome this trend and seek to make space for unique and relevant skills and attributes. So, when applying to Leadership Connect we focus on evaluation based on key competencies needed for success in the position. We look forward to receiving your application!

Leadership Connect is committed to creating a diverse environment and is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Head of People
description:
About Bizee

Bizee has helped nearly two million entrepreneurs start and run their businesses. Now we are building what comes next: an AI-powered Entrepreneur Operating System that helps founders launch, operate, stay compliant, and grow with less friction and more confidence.

We are profitable, founder-led, and operating at a clear inflection point. The next chapter requires a stronger operating system for how we hire, lead, communicate, develop talent, and scale execution across the company.

The Opportunity

We are looking for a Head of People & Organizational Effectiveness to serve as a strategic partner to the President and executive team.

This is not a maintenance HR role. It is a builder-operator role for someone who can create clarity, raise the talent bar, strengthen the leadership layer, and build the people systems that help Bizee scale without unnecessary complexity or organizational drag.

As Bizee grows and transforms, one of the most important responsibilities of this role will be building and protecting a high talent bar. We are not simply adding headcount; we are intentionally increasing talent density across the company. This leader will help ensure we hire high-caliber builders, operators, and leaders who raise the standard, strengthen execution, and fit the culture we are building.

You will help design how Bizee hires, onboards, develops, manages, communicates, and performs as we move into our next stage of growth.

What You Will Own

  • Partner with the President and executive team on org design, leadership effectiveness, talent quality, succession, performance, and cross-functional friction
  • Design and protect Bizee's talent bar. Build the hiring standards, role scorecards, interview calibration, decision rules, and leadership alignment required to consistently identify high-caliber talent as the company scales. Ensure we do not compromise on quality-of-hire in the name of speed
  • Design a scalable hiring system, including workforce planning, role definition, scorecards, interview loops, calibration, decision frameworks, and candidate experience
  • Build Bizee's People Operating System, including the rhythms, mechanisms, and rituals that drive alignment, accountability, and performance
  • Strengthen the manager layer through practical enablement, feedback systems, performance routines, and leadership expectations
  • Design and own Bizee's performance cycle: goal setting, ratings, calibration, and linkage to comp and promotion decisions
  • Advise on role clarity, leveling, compensation philosophy, incentive design, spans, layers, and capability planning
  • Build onboarding, engagement, and internal communication mechanisms that help employees ramp faster and stay connected to the mission
  • Lead change management as Bizee modernizes its platform, operating model, AI-enabled workflows, and distributed teams
  • Plan and execute the eventual transition away from full co-employment (PEO) arrangements in multiple countries globally, including building the internal HRIS, payroll, benefits and compliance infrastructure needed to stand up direct employment, sequencing the migration to minimize risk and disruption, and managing the associated legal, tax, and employee communication considerations.
  • Own multi-state wage and hour compliance and workforce administration for a distributed employee population spanning nearly all 50 U.S. states, encompassing both exempt and non-exempt staff
  • Lead and integrate teams across multiple countries internationally, ensuring a single coherent Bizee culture and set of operating standards translate effectively across geographies, management structures, and local labor norms

Requirements

What You Will Bring

  • 10+ years of progressive experience in People, Talent, HRBP, Organizational Effectiveness, or People Operations leadership
  • Experience building People systems from the ground up, ideally in a high-growth, profitable, founder-led, PE-backed, SaaS, platform, marketplace, or tech-enabled services environment
  • Strong executive judgment and the ability to operate as a true strategic partner to a President and executive team
  • Proven experience with hiring systems, talent calibration, performance management, manager enablement, org design, leveling, compensation frameworks, and People Ops infrastructure
  • A strong point of view on talent density, quality-of-hire, leadership standards, and what it takes to build a high-performance company
  • Strong operator instincts: structured, practical, clear, and biased toward action
  • High EQ and high backbone: able to coach leaders, challenge assumptions, handle sensitive issues, and create clarity without adding drama or bureaucracy
  • Strategic and hands-on: able to advise the executive team in the morning and fix an onboarding workflow in the afternoon
  • Business-first orientation: understands revenue, margin, productivity, execution, and the tradeoffs of scaling a profitable company
  • Low-drama, high-signal communication style: brings facts, judgment, candor, and calm to complex situations
  • Culture carrier: protects what makes Bizee special while raising the performance bar
  • Comfort operating in ambiguity and building while the business is moving fast
  • Strong written communication skills and the ability to turn messy organizational issues into simple plans and operating mechanisms
  • Demonstrated experience managing a multi-state U.S. workforce (ideally 40+ states) that includes both exempt and non-exempt employees, with working knowledge of state-by-state wage and hour variation and how to operationalize compliance without adding unnecessary drag
  • Experience leading or partnering with teams in multiple countries, with a track record of integrating culture, standards, and management practices across borders rather than running parallel, disconnected functions
  • Direct experience transitioning a workforce out of a co-employment/PEO model, including standing up in-country or in-state direct employment infrastructure (payroll, benefits, statutory compliance) and managing the change process for affected employees and managers

What Success Looks Like

  • In the first year, Bizee has a stronger People Operating System that improves alignment, ownership, speed, and accountability across the company
  • Hiring is more structured, faster, and higher-signal. Leaders know what great looks like by role and level, hiring decisions are more disciplined, and the company is increasing talent density as it scales
  • Managers are better equipped to set direction, coach performance, handle conflict, and create clarity. Employees have stronger expectations, better communication, and a clearer understanding of how their work connects to the company's mission

The Head of People & Organizational Effectiveness becomes a trusted advisor to the President, executive team, and leadership layer, helping Bizee scale with discipline, clarity, high standards, and good judgment.

Benefits

Disclaimer:

Bizee is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with Bizee is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

The compensation offered to the successful applicant may vary based on factors including experience, skills, education, location, and other job-related reasons.

This employer participates in E-verify and will provide the federal government with your Form I-9 information to confirm that you are authorized to work in the U.S.

Available benefits include: Health Care Plan (Medical, Dental & Vision), Life Insurance (Basic, Voluntary & AD&D), Virtual Wellness Resources, Work From Home

title: Testing Recommendation Step
description:
At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world's most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good.

Your impact

test

Here's what you'll need

test

Our health and welfare benefits are designed to invest in you, and in the things you care about. Your health. Your well-being. Your security. Your future. title: Backend Developer
description:
albo

albo is a leading fintech company offering financial products to individuals and SMBs with the mission to bring financial freedom to everyone everywhere.

About The Role

As a Senior Backend Engineer you'll design, build, and operate the microservices that power albo's core financial products â from card issuing and authorization to credit lines, payments, and money movement. You'll own services end-to-end across our polyglot, event-driven platform, making pragmatic architectural decisions that keep money-critical flows correct, idempotent, and observable at scale. You'll work close to the domain (SPEI, ledgers, credit, authorization) and close to production, partnering with Product, Infra, and other engineering squads to ship reliable financial infrastructure.

What Youâll Be Doing At Albo

  • Design, build, and maintain backend microservices in Go and Java/Quarkus within our domain-oriented architecture (customer, account, transactional, and shared domains).
  • Own services end-to-end: architecture, implementation, testing, deployment to QA/SBX/PROD, monitoring, and on-call ownership of what you ship.
  • Build event-driven and REST integrations across services (Pub/Sub, service-to-service REST via our private-proxy mesh), with strong guarantees around idempotency, reconciliation, and failure handling (dead-letter/reprocess, no silent data loss).
  • Model and evolve data across MongoDB and SingleStore/MemSQL, keeping dual-write flows consistent between the display source of truth and what card authorization actually validates.
  • Ship through our CI/CD (Jenkins shared pipelines, containerized deploys to GKE/Kubernetes on GCP), managing configuration and secrets via GCP Secret Manager.
  • Write clean, self-documenting, well-tested code; participate in code reviews and raise the engineering bar for correctness and simplicity.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Infra/Platform, and other backend squads to break down epics into shippable, incremental deliveries.
  • Instrument observability (structured logging, metrics, tracing) and use it to debug production issues and continuously improve reliability and performance.
  • Contribute to technical design docs, architecture diagrams, and shared conventions/scaffolding that scale the team.

About You And What Type Of Skills You'll Need

  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field (or equivalent hands-on experience).
  • +5 years of experience building and operating backend services in production, ideally in the Banking or Fintech industry.
  • Strong proficiency in at least one of Go or Java (Quarkus/Spring or similar), and willingness to work across both in a polyglot environment.
  • Solid experience with microservices and distributed systems: REST APIs, asynchronous/event-driven messaging (Pub/Sub, Kafka, or similar), service boundaries, and versioned contracts.
  • Hands-on experience with cloud infrastructure (GCP preferred), containers and Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines.
  • Comfort with both SQL and NoSQL data stores (MongoDB, SingleStore/MySQL/Postgres) and an eye for data consistency in money-critical flows.
  • Deep understanding of reliability patterns â idempotency, retries, reconciliation, graceful degradation â and why they matter when handling real money.
  • Familiarity with fintech/payments concepts (card authorization, ledgers, transfers, credit) is a strong plus.
  • Customer Centricity: You're customer-obsessed, constantly thinking about improving the customer experience.
  • Hands-on: We're looking for a comfortable and willing leader to be a hands-on contributor who is energized by rolling up their sleeves, making things happen, and enabling the team to do the same.
  • Done is better than perfect: As things change and move quickly, you are excited by the fast pace and opportunity to constantly learn and help your team learn.
  • Communication: Excellent ability to communicate internally and externally, quickly build relationships, and work cross-functionally.
  • Extreme ownership: You hold yourself accountable to a high bar. You are supremely organized and see what needs to happen to achieve goals.
  • Results & Data Driven: You understand the business metrics you are responsible for, and you demonstrate these insights to drive constant improvement.

title: Freelance Creative Operations Coordinator
description:
The Klick Groupâcomprising Klick Health (including Klick Katalyst and btwelve), Klick Media Group, Klick Applied Sciences (including Klick Labs), Klick Consulting, and Klick Venturesâis an ecosystem dedicated to realizing the full potential of our people and clients in life sciences. Consistently named a Best Managed Company, Most Admired Corporate Culture, and Great Place to Work, Klick is committed to fostering a high-performance, people-first culture with creativity, collaboration, innovation, and impact across everything we do.

About Our Creative Craft

Our Creative Craft brings Klickâs ideas to life through storytelling, design, and experience. This craft blends imagination with strategy to create work that is emotionally resonant and culturally relevant. Creative teams collaborate across disciplines to simplify complexity and humanize healthcare. Their work drives engagement, understanding, and meaningful action across channels.

Job Description

As a key member of the Creative Operations and Resourcing group, you will report to the Senior Creative Resource Manager and play a vital role in keeping processes on track. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys administrative work, has strong communication and organizational skills, and wants to contribute to a dynamic creative environment.

Position type: freelance

Hours per week: expected 30 - 35 hours

Duration: 3 months to start, possibility to extend

What You'll Do

  • Support the high-volume invoicing process, ensuring accuracy and timeliness.
  • Provide Production Resource Management coverage as needed.
  • Provide administrative support for the internal resource management system.
  • Follow up on time entry to help ensure reporting is completed on time.
  • Provide administrative support for weekly resourcing meetings.
  • Manage and update documents, maintain organized filing systems, and keep folder structures clear and consistent.
  • Jump in wherever needed to keep operations running smoothly and efficiently.

Qualifications

  • Strong organizational skills and exceptional attention to detail.
  • Comfort using Slack, Zoom and Google Workspace tools, including Gmail, Google Drive, and Google Docs/Sheets.
  • Clear, professional written and verbal communication skills.
  • A proactive mindsetâyou identify what needs to be done and take initiative.
  • Experience in a creative, agency, or operations environment is an asset

Additional Information

The hourly range for this position is between CAD $40/hour and CAD $45/hour. The final amount offered will be determined based on several factors such as a candidate's work location, their unique skill set, education, and prior work experience.

Our Commitment to Inclusion

Klick is consciously creating a culture where everyone can thrive and grow in their careers. We believe that our best work comes from our diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills. We strongly encourage members of historically underrepresented communities to apply, including Black people, Indigenous peoples, and other people of color, people with disabilities, people from gender and sexually diverse communities and people with intersectional identities. We're also committed to developing an inclusive, barrier-free recruitment process and work environment. Should you require any accommodation, please contact us at careers@klick.com and we will work with you to meet your accessibility needs and ensure you have a positive experience.

title: PR-Redakteur & Content Manager (m/w/d)
description:
HEYST? Wirkt gut! Nicht nur für unsere Kunden, sondern auch für dich und deine Karriere! Denn bei uns geht was: Wir sind eine der führenden Agenturen in Essen und entwickeln wirkungsvolle Kommunikationslösungen. Und suchen kreative Köpfe, gewiefte Wortakrobaten und erfahrene Organisationstalente, die mit uns weiterwachsen wollen. Ergo: Leute wie Dich! Also komm ruhig näher ran und schau, was wir für Dich haben!

Aufgaben

Du bist ein kommunikatives Schweizer Messer mit einer Leidenschaft für B2B-Themen? Du liebst es, komplexe Sachverhalte auf den Punkt zu bringen und weißt genau, wie man Geschichten für anspruchsvolle Zielgruppen erzählt – egal ob in der Wirtschafts- oder Tagespresse, auf digitalen Kanälen oder in einem Video? Dann bist Du bei uns genau richtig! Als PR-Redakteur / Content Manager (m/w/d) bist Du bei uns nicht nur Texter/in, sondern auch Stratege/in, Berater/in und Projektmanager/in.

Das machst Du bei uns:

  • Du entwickelst und steuerst die B2B-PR für unsere Kunden und baust proaktiv Kontakte zu Redaktionen der Tages- und Wirtschaftspresse auf und aus.
  • Du arbeitest Dich schnell in komplexe Sachverhalte ein und bereitest diese zielgruppengerecht für alle Kanäle (Print & Digital) auf.
  • Du erstellst vielfältigen Content – von Pressemitteilungen über Fachartikel bis hin zu Social-Media-Posts und Videokonzepten.
  • Als KI-Native setzt Du nicht nur die gängigen Tools souverän ein, sondern optimierst auch unsere redaktionellen Workflows und findest neue Wege der Content-Produktion.
  • Du managst souverän Deine Projekte: von der Angebotserstellung über die Kundenberatung in allen Kommunikationsfragen bis hin zur Abrechnung.
  • Du hast aktuelle GEO/SEO/SEA-Trends im Blick und lässt Dein Wissen in die Content-Erstellung einfließen, um die digitale Sichtbarkeit unserer Kunden zu maximieren.

Qualifikation

  • Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung in der PR und Redaktion, idealerweise in einer B2B-Agentur oder einem B2B-Unternehmen.
  • Ein nachweisbares Netzwerk zu Journalisten und Medien der Tages- und Wirtschaftspresse.
  • Exzellente redaktionelle Fähigkeiten und die Fertigkeit, komplexe Themen einfach und verständlich zu erklären.
  • Belegbare Erfahrung mit gängigen KI-Anwendungen im Redaktions- und Content-Bereich.
  • Gute Kenntnisse in den Bereichen SEO/SEA sowie Erfahrung in der Konzeption von Video-Content.
  • ...

Benefits

  • Viel Freiraum, Deine eigenen Ideen auszuleben und bei uns einzubringen.
  • Spannende Projekte, interessante Kunden und ein großes Netzwerk.
  • Flexible Arbeitszeiten ohne ständige Allnighter und Wochenendarbeit.
  • Drei Tage mobiles Arbeiten pro Woche.
  • Dein ganz persönliches Fördermodell mit Weiterentwicklungsmöglichkeiten.
  • Spannende Kooperationsprojekte mit den Verticals ERROR, DIP und GesundHEYST unserer Agenturfamilie.
  • ... und vieles mehr, was wir Dir gerne persönlich erzählen!

Alles weitere gibt`s im persönlichen Gespräch. Wir freuen uns auf Deine Bewerbung!

title: Designer Retail & Interior (m/w/d)
description:
Gestalte keine Flyer. Gestalte Räume.

Es gibt Designer, deren Arbeit verschwindet nach ein paar Tagen im Papierkorb.

Und es gibt Designer, deren Ideen später in Supermärkten, Hotels, Serviced Apartments, Restaurants oder modernen Arbeitswelten Realität werden.

Zu welcher Gruppe möchtest du gehören?

Wir sind WESPRO. Wir entwickeln und realisieren individuelle Konzepte für Retail Design, Commercial Spaces, Hospitality und Interior Design – von der ersten Idee über die Produktion bis zur Montage. Alles unter einem Dach.

Genau dafür suchen wir einen Designer, der nicht in DIN A4 denkt, sondern in Räumen.

Aufgaben

Das erwartet dich

Du entwickelst Gestaltungskonzepte, die später tatsächlich gebaut werden.

Du arbeitest nicht nur am Bildschirm, sondern begleitest Projekte von der ersten Idee bis zur fertigen Umsetzung.

Kein Projekt gleicht dem anderen.

Mal entsteht eine komplette Verkaufsfläche, mal ein Hotelkonzept, mal ein Showroom oder eine Markenwelt.

Du arbeitest eng mit unserem Projektmanagement, unserer Produktion und Montage zusammen und siehst jeden Tag, wie aus Ideen Realität wird.

Qualifikation

Das bringst du mit

  • Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung im Designbereich

  • Sehr gute Kenntnisse der Adobe Creative Cloud

  • Räumliches Denken und ein gutes Gespür für Gestaltung

  • Interesse an Retail Design, Interior Design oder Architektur

  • Eigeninitiative und Lust, Verantwortung zu übernehmen

  • Freude daran, im Team außergewöhnliche Projekte umzusetzen

Benefits

Warum WESPRO?

Weil wir keine klassische Werbeagentur sind.

Bei uns entstehen keine Standardlösungen.

Wir entwickeln individuelle Projekte für namhafte Unternehmen und begleiten diese bis zur fertigen Umsetzung.

Du arbeitest in einem Team mit kurzen Entscheidungswegen, viel Eigenverantwortung und abwechslungsreichen Projekten.

Natürlich gehören auch kostenlose Getränke, Kaffee, regelmäßige Firmenevents und kostenlose Parkplätze dazu.

Unser Büro befindet sich in Berlin-Bohnsdorf und ist sowohl mit dem Auto (Nähe Stadtautobahn) als auch mit den öffentlichen Verkehrsmitteln gut erreichbar.

Klingt nach dir?

Dann freuen wir uns darauf, dich kennenzulernen.

title: Sachbearbeiter:in Marktforschung (m/w/d)
description:
Du arbeitest gerne mit Zahlen, hast ein Auge fürs Detail und möchtest aus Befragungsdaten wertvolle Erkenntnisse gewinnen? Dann bist du bei uns genau richtig! Du begleitest Befragungen und Produkttest-Kampagnen von der Fragebogenerstellung bis zur Auswertung und unterstützt unsere Teams mit fundierten Analysen.

Als führendes Verbraucherportal für neue Lebensmittel und Markenprodukte inspiriert brandnooz seit über 17 Jahren Food-Fans in ganz Deutschland. Mit unseren Lebensmittelboxen und Produkttest-Kampagnen bringen wir neue Trends direkt nach Hause und verknüpfen mehr als 1.000 Unternehmen aus der Lebensmittel- und Konsumgüterbranche mit unserer Community aus 480.000 Noozies.

Im Bereich Marktforschung suchen wir ab sofort Unterstützung in Vollzeit. Vielleicht können wir dich demnächst schon als Neuzugang in unserem Foodieversum willkommen heißen.

Aufgaben

  • Du erstellst und programmierst Online-Fragebögen für unsere Box-Befragungen, Produkttests sowie weitere interne und externe Marktforschungsprojekte.
  • Du betreust quantitative Befragungen von der Vorbereitung über die Durchführung bis zum Abschluss.
  • Du entwickelst und analysierst Befragungen zu unternehmensrelevanten Themen wie Sortimentsplanung oder die Weiterentwicklung von Angeboten.
  • Du prüfst eingehende Daten auf Vollständigkeit, Plausibilität und Qualität.
  • Du bereitest die Ergebnisse übersichtlich in Tabellen, Auswertungen, Reportings und Präsentationen für unsere Kunden und internen Teams auf.
  • Du arbeitest eng mit unserem Projektmanagement und weiteren internen Teams zusammen und unterstützt sie mit datenbasierten Insights bei Entscheidungen.
  • Du bringst dich in die Weiterentwicklung unserer Fragebogen-, Auswertungs- und Reporting-Prozesse ein.

Qualifikation

  • Du hast eine kaufmännische Ausbildung, Berufserfahrung im kaufmännischen Bereich oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation.
  • Du arbeitest gerne mit Zahlen, erkennst Zusammenhänge und leitest daraus relevante Erkenntnisse ab.
  • Sehr gute Kenntnisse in Microsoft Excel und PowerPoint setzen wir voraus.
  • Du arbeitest sorgfältig, strukturiert und zuverlässig und behältst auch bei mehreren parallelen Projekten den Überblick.
  • Außerdem bist du lösungsorientiert und bringst gerne proaktiv eigene Ideen ein.
  • Erste Erfahrungen in der Marktforschung, mit Umfragetools, Reportings, Datenanalysen oder Kalkulationen sind ein Plus.
  • Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift setzen wir voraus; Englischkenntnisse sind von Vorteil.
  • Du erfüllst nicht alle Punkte? Kein Problem – wir freuen uns auf deine Bewerbung und dein Potenzial!

  • Flexible Arbeitszeiten und eine Kernarbeitszeit von 10 bis 16 Uhr.
  • Bis zu 8 Tage Homeoffice im Monat und bis zu 2 Wochen Workation im Jahr.
  • Büro in Ottensen mit Kaffee und Softdrinks en masse sowie frischem Obst.

Können wir dich dafür begeistern, Teil des brandnooz Teams zu werden und aus Verbraucherfeedback wertvolle Insights für spannende Marken, Produkte und unsere eigenen Angebote zu gewinnen? Dann freuen wir uns darauf, dich kennenzulernen.

Schick uns einfach deinen Lebenslauf und ein paar Zeilen zu dir inklusive deiner Gehaltsvorstellungen und deines frühesten Eintrittsdatums an Linda Hofhus.

title: Qualitätsingenieur (m/w/d) Wareneingangsdokumente und QM-System
description:
Wir entwickeln und fertigen Radsätze und deren Komponenten mit der höchsten Qualität und für alle Arten von Schienenfahrzeugen: klassische Züge und Hochgeschwindigkeitszüge, U-Bahnen, Straßenbahnen und Nahverkehrswagen, Lokomotiven und Güterwagen. Und liefern in weltweit mehr als 80 Länder.

Aufgaben

  • Überprüfen von Wareneingangszeugnissen (Maßprotokolle, Materialzeugnisse, Prüfnachweise usw.) gemäß Anforderung auf Vollständigkeit und Plausibilität, sowie entsprechende Freigabe der Dokumentation im System
  • Anfordern von fehlenden Nachweisen oder Aufforderung zur Korrektur von fehlerhaften Dokumentationen beim Lieferanten
  • In Grenzfällen Beratung mit den Fachabteilungen und Zulieferern in Fragen der werkstofftechnischen Eigenschaften von Materialien
  • Sicherstellung, Pflege und kontinuierliche Verbesserung des integrierten Managementsystems (IMS) unter Berücksichtigung gesetzlicher, normativer und kundenspezifischer Anforderungen (z.B. ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 50001 und ISO 22163)

title: Senior DevOps/ Cloud Engineer (f/m/d)
description:
Become a Part of ACCURIDS

Join us and help shape the future of pharmaceutical data!

At ACCURIDS, we’re building the data backbone behind life-science innovation — a system that identifies, links, and standardizes critical enterprise data in compliance with global standards such as ISO IDMP. We help global life-science companies launch products faster, more safely, and with better patient outcomes.

At ACCURIDS, you’ll join a collaborative, growth-driven environment where your work makes a real impact from day one.

How you’ll make an impact:

  • You set up, maintain, and troubleshoot customer installations, managing both high-standard SaaS environments and restrictive customer on-premise deployments.
  • You operate and scale our core AWS infrastructure, managing multi-account environments, EKS, RDS, S3, networking, and IAM.
  • You support our development team with product operations, maintaining and extending GitLab CI/CD pipelines, driving declarative GitOps workflows (ArgoCD/Kargo), and managing incident response.
  • You develop the Accurids SaaS platform stack, focusing on enhancing platform automation, Infrastructure as Code (IaC), identity management, and advanced observability.
  • You drive our security operations, building out our compliance posture through log reviews, vulnerability scanning, active monitoring, alerting, and supporting ISO 27001 requirements.
  • You ensure operational resilience and continuity, working as a key senior pillar to transition our infrastructure from a single-point-of-failure risk to a robust, well-documented architecture.

What helps you succeed:

  • Adaptability & Continuous Learning: A strong engineering mindset with a proven ability to pick up new tools, architectures, and methodologies rapidly as our ecosystem evolves.
  • Cloud & Infrastructure Foundation: 3–5+ years of experience in DevOps, Cloud Engineering, or Platform Engineering, with production exposure to a major cloud provider (we use AWS, but Azure or GCP experience is just as welcome).
  • Containerization Concepts: Solid foundational knowledge of the Kubernetes ecosystem, Docker containerization, and configuring CI/CD pipelines (such as GitLab CI).
  • Modern Operations (Our Stack): Familiarity with—or a strong desire to learn—Infrastructure as Code (Terraform) and declarative GitOps workflows (ArgoCD/Kargo).
  • Observability & Security Awareness: Observability & Security: Familiarity with cloud security tooling and monitoring/observability (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana, Elasticsearch). Experience building a security posture is a strong plus.
  • Core Competencies: Business fluency in English and an openness to integrating AI-assisted tools (like Cursor, Copilot, or ChatGPT) into your daily scripting and debugging workflows. (It’s a nice-to-have bonus if you have prior experience with Elasticsearch, Keycloak, Spring Boot, PostgreSQL, or compliance frameworks like ISO 27001!)

Why you’ll love working here:

  • Hybrid work model: Work from our Cologne office with flexible hybrid arrangements.
  • 30 days of paid vacation for a healthy work-life balance.
  • Competitive salary package aligned with your experience and impact.
  • Personal growth and development: Access a flexible budget for training, conferences, or other learning opportunities that support your individual development goals.
  • Choose your perk: Enjoy either a subsidized Urban Sports Club membership or a City Plus Card with a €50 monthly allowance.
  • Empowerment and ownership: Take responsibility and shape your role as we grow together.
  • Modern work setup: Select your preferred system and hardware – Windows, Linux, or Mac.
  • Collaborative culture: Be part of a dynamic, start-up-like environment that values flexibility, trust, and innovation.

Not ticking every box?

We encourage you to apply anyway! We know great talent doesn’t always follow a checklist and we care more about your potential, curiosity, and motivation to learn.

At ACCURIDS, we’re committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. We especially encourage women and people from underrepresented backgrounds in tech, science, and data to apply.

title: Senior Full Stack Developer | Java / React (f/m/d)
description:
Become a Part of ACCURIDS

Join us and help shape the future of pharmaceutical data!

At ACCURIDS, we’re building the data backbone behind life-science innovation — a system that identifies, links, and standardizes critical enterprise data in compliance with global standards such as ISO IDMP. We help global life-science companies launch products faster, more safely, and with better patient outcomes.

At ACCURIDS, you’ll join a collaborative, growth-driven environment where your work makes a real impact from day one.

As a key member of our Product Development team, you will operate with a high degree of autonomy while collaborating closely with backend and frontend engineers, QA, DevOps, and product owners. In this role, you will directly align with cross-functional engineering stakeholders and indirectly impact our customers through the features and fixes you deliver, while having direct access to our CTO as a trusted mentor and technical sparring partner

How you’ll make an impact:

  • End-to-End Feature Development: Deliver product features across the full stack, seamlessly moving between our React/TypeScript frontend and modern Java/Spring Boot backend, including the GraphQL and REST API layers in between. You take independent ownership of codebase areas from refinement to production.
  • AI-Driven Velocity & Innovation: Work by default in an AI-augmented way, using modern AI coding agents and tools like Cursor and Claude Code to increase output and deliver features faster. You will push the limits of agentic workflows, mastering next-generation developer tools to keep our AI-native engineering team at the forefront of software innovation.
  • Backend Engineering: Implement robust services utilizing Spring Boot, manage JPA/Hibernate against PostgreSQL with Flyway migrations, optimize Elasticsearch queries, and dive into semantic data structures using the Apache Jena RDF triplestore.
  • Frontend Engineering: Build responsive React components and manage global state with Redux, fetching data with Apollo Client and implementing advanced data visualizations like D3 or Recharts optimized for large datasets.
  • Quality Assurance & Testing: Uphold rigorous testing standards using JUnit, Mockito, Testcontainers, and WireMock on the backend, alongside Jest, Testing Library, Cypress, and Playwright on the frontend to maintain our SonarQube quality gate.
  • Architecture & Modularization: Help carve complex features into clean modules, reduce technical debt, and actively contribute to our ongoing evolution toward a multi-module Spring Modulith architecture.
  • Code Review & Mentoring: Serve as an autonomous technical reference point within the team by reviewing merge requests, sharing domain knowledge, and mentoring less experienced engineers.

What helps you succeed:

  • Experience: 5+ years of professional software development experience shipping and owning features end-to-end within a production SaaS product.
  • AI Tooling Curiosity: Extreme comfort and enthusiasm for experimenting with next-generation agentic workflows, using tools like Cursor and Claude Code to accelerate scaffolding, refactoring, and debugging. You view AI as an opportunity, not a threat.
  • Backend Mastery: Deep proficiency in modern Java (17+) and the Spring Boot ecosystem (Spring Security, Spring Data JPA), alongside relational databases (PostgreSQL) and REST/GraphQL API design.
  • Frontend Expertise: Strong frontend engineering skills utilizing React 18, TypeScript, Redux state management, and Apollo Client.
  • Testing & Tools: Complete fluency in testing across the full stack and managing Git-based workflows within a GitLab CI/CD environment.
  • Core Competencies: Business fluency in English, an exceptional solution-oriented mindset, and a genuine curiosity to adapt quickly to new domain-heavy tools and architectures.
  • Nice-to-haves: It’s a major plus if you have experience with semantic web technologies (RDF, SPARQL, Apache Jena), Material UI (MUI 5), Elasticsearch, or a basic awareness of cloud/Kubernetes operations. Prior exposure to the life sciences or pharmaceutical domains (like FHIR or IDMP) is a bonus.

Why you’ll love working here:

  • Hybrid work model: Work from our Cologne office with flexible hybrid arrangements.
  • 30 days of paid vacation for a healthy work-life balance.
  • Competitive salary package: A fixed salary package of €75,000 – €100,000 per year, aligned with your level experience and impact in this permanent, full-time role.
  • Personal growth and development: Access a flexible budget for training, conferences, or other learning opportunities that support your individual development goals.
  • Choose your perk: Enjoy either a subsidized Urban Sports Club membership or a City Plus Card with a €50 monthly allowance.
  • Empowerment and ownership: Take responsibility and shape your role as we grow together.
  • Modern work setup: Select your preferred system and hardware – Windows, Linux, or Mac.
  • Collaborative culture: Be part of a dynamic, start-up-like environment that values flexibility, trust, and innovation.

Not ticking every box?

Not ticking every box? We encourage you to apply anyway! We know great talent doesn’t always follow a checklist and we care more about your potential, curiosity, and motivation to learn.

At ACCURIDS, we’re committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. We especially encourage women and people from underrepresented backgrounds in tech, science, and data to apply.

Affinity ist ein globales AdTech-Unternehmen mit über 600 Mitarbeitenden in Europa, den USA und Asien. Mit unseren Plattformen Opinary und mCanvas unterstützen wir Marken dabei, ihre Zielgruppen mit innovativen digitalen Werbelösungen zu erreichen.

Als Specialist Account Management bist du die Schnittstelle zwischen unseren Kunden und internen Teams. Du begleitest Kampagnen, analysierst Ergebnisse und unterstützt Kunden dabei, unsere Produkte Opinary und mCanvas erfolgreich einzusetzen.

  • Du betreust Kunden im europäischen Markt und bist Ansprechperson für laufende Kampagnen und Projekte

title: Finance Operations Manager (m/w/d)
description:
Wir sind Start to Finish.

Kein Startup kann wachsen ohne das richtige Team. Aus diesem Grund unterstützen wir aufstrebende Startups und Growth Companies wie Snocks, SellerX, Formary, Schema M und viele weitere dabei, ihre Schlüsselpositionen mit den richtigen Talenten zu besetzen.

Mit über 100 zufriedenen Kunden sind wir die #1 Recruiting Boutique für E-Commerce Startups im deutschsprachigen Raum.

Unser Kunde sucht Dich!

Einer der bekanntesten Online-Händler für KFZ-Ersatzteile in Deutschland – gewachsen ohne externe Investoren, dafür mit klarem Fokus auf Service, Qualität und schlanke Prozesse. Über 150.000 Artikel von Premium-Herstellern, passgenaue Teilezuordnung und eine schnelle Logistik machen ihn für Endkunden, freie Werkstätten und Händler zum verlässlichen Partner im freien Ersatzteilmarkt.

Aktuell wird von 15 auf 20 Mio. € Umsatz skaliert – und mit dem Wachstum steigen die Anforderungen an saubere Daten und reibungslose Abläufe. Genau dafür suchen wir Dich:
Du bist zentrale Ansprechperson für Qualität und Richtigkeit im IT-System was die Buchhaltung betrifft, koordinierst die Retouren, stehst im engen Austausch mit den Lieferanten und sorgst für reibungslose Abläufe im Unternehmen – methodisch, lösungsorientiert und mit dem Blick fürs Ganze.

Aufgaben

  • Kaufmännische Abwicklung: Du verantwortest Gutschriften, Stornos und Rechnungskorrekturen und arbeitest eng mit Debitoren- und Kreditorenthemen.
  • Qualitätssicherung im System: Du bist zentrale Ansprechperson für Qualität und Richtigkeit in unserem IT-System – du prüfst, pflegst und hältst die Daten sauber.
  • Retourenmanagement: Du koordinierst die Retouren end-to-end und sorgst für eine reibungslose Abwicklung.
  • Schadens- und Reklamationsbearbeitung: Du bearbeitest Schadensfälle und Reklamationen strukturiert und lösungsorientiert.
  • Lieferantenkommunikation: Du stehst im engen Austausch mit unseren Lieferanten und klärst offene Punkte eigenständig.
  • Prozessoptimierung: Du erkennst, wo es hakt – und bringst Vorschläge ein, statt nur zu reagieren.

Qualifikation

  • Eine abgeschlossene kaufmännische Ausbildung oder vergleichbare Erfahrung
  • Berufserfahrung in kaufmännischer Sachbearbeitung, Buchhaltung, Retouren- oder Reklamationsmanagement
  • Sicherer Umgang mit ERP-/Warenwirtschaftssystemen und ein Auge für Datenqualität
  • Eine strukturierte, methodische Arbeitsweise und den Blick fürs Ganze
  • Kommunikationsstärke im Austausch mit Lieferanten und internen Teams
  • Eigenverantwortung und die Fähigkeit, dich im Remote-Setting selbst zu organisieren

Von Vorteil: Erfahrung im E-Commerce oder im Handel mit KFZ-Teilen.

Benefits

  • 100 % remote – arbeite von überall, ohne Pendeln
  • Ein wachsendes Unternehmen mit kurzen Wegen und schnellen Entscheidungen
  • Viel Gestaltungsspielraum: Du baust Prozesse mit auf, statt sie nur zu verwalten
  • Ein Team, das mitdenkt und anpackt
  • Unbefristete Festanstellung in Vollzeit

🚀 Bereit für den nächsten Step?

Dann bewirb dich jetzt - Wir freuen uns auf dich! 💡

Die angegebene Gehaltsrange basiert auf aktuellen Marktdaten und gibt dir eine realistische Orientierung. Am Ende hängt es aber von zwei Dingen ab: deiner tatsächlichen Qualifikation – und vor allem von unserem Kunden, der die finale Entscheidung trifft. Wir sind eine Vermittlung, kein Arbeitgeber – das heißt, wir können das Angebot nicht selbst festlegen.
Was wir aber können: dich bestmöglich beraten, dein Profil stark positionieren und gemeinsam mit dir schauen, was drin ist.

title: Data Engineer
description:
As part of the Platform tribe, you will help build a self-service, AI-Ready Data Platform. The Data Platform team supports our next big bets: Data, AI, and real-time analytics. We are a team focused on building scalable, reliable infrastructure to enable our global data community.

What you'll do

  • Build and maintain best-in-class data infrastructure for critical processes.
  • Develop features that strengthen self-service, including for ETL and orchestration.
  • Optimise processes to use compute more efficiently and reduce cost.
  • Automate operations so the platform stays highly available.
  • Contribute to the design and implementation of new services.

You'll be a strong fit if you

  • Have experience with infrastructure as code and containerisation (e.g. Terraform, Helm, Docker, Kubernetes).
  • Have used modern query engines (e.g. Snowflake, Databricks, BigQuery, Trino).
  • Have worked with modern data orchestration tools (e.g. Airflow, Dagster).
  • Are proficient in at least one of our main languages: Python or Go.
  • Bring a pragmatic, team-first mindset.
  • Are self-motivated, work well independently, and can balance stakeholder needs.

Nice to have 

  • Experience building and operating performant API layers.
  • Experience with dbt for transform, test, and documentation workflows.
  • Query optimization at scale.
  • Real-time streaming (e.g. Kafka, Flink, StarRocks, Pinot, Druid, ClickHouse).
  • Experience with systems at petabyte scale.
  • Experience implementing AI-enabled systems (e.g. agentic SRE, AI automation).

About SumUp 

We believe in the everyday hero. Small business owners are at the heart of all we do, so we're creating tools that help them run their businesses. With a founder's mentality and a 'team-first' attitude, our diverse teams across Europe, South America, and the United States work together to ensure that the small business owners we partner with can be successful doing what they love. SumUp is an Equal Employment Opportunity employer that proudly pursues and hires a diverse workforce. SumUp does not make hiring or employment decisions on the basis of race, colour, religion or religious belief, ethnic or national origin, nationality, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, age or any other basis protected by applicable laws or prohibited by company policy. SumUp also strives for a healthy and safe workplace and strictly prohibits harassment of any kind. SumUp will not accept unsolicited resumes from any source other than directly from a candidate. 

Job Application Tip

We recognise that candidates feel they need to meet 100% of the job criteria in order to apply for a job. Please note that this is only a guide. If you don't tick every box, it's ok too because it means you have room to learn and develop your career at SumUp.

title: Praktikum Brand & Product Management (m/w/d)
description:
Du liebst Pflegeprodukte und Markenaufbau, denkst lösungsorientiert und hast Lust, an der Schnittstelle zwischen Produktentwicklung und Brand Strategy extrem schnell zu lernen und Dinge wirklich auf die Straße zu bringen? Dann wirst Du Dich hier wohlfühlen.

Unser Ziel:

Unsere Marke von einer reinen Rasurmarke zu einer ganzheitlichen Men Wellbeing Brand weiterzuentwickeln – mit innovativen Produktkategorien, einem modernen Markenauftritt und einem starken Kern-Team. Wenn Du nicht nur zuschauen, sondern aktiv mitgestalten willst, lies weiter.

Deine Mission:

Du unterstützt unser Brand- & Produkt-Team direkt im operativen Tagesgeschäft und übernimmst eigene Teilprojekte. Du schlägst die Brücke zwischen Produktentwicklung und Markenführung. Statt stupide Listen abzuarbeiten, hast Du bei uns den Freiraum, eigene Lösungen einzubringen, Produkte mitzugestalten und extrem schnell Erfahrung in unterschiedlichen E-Commerce-Bereiche zu sammeln.

Aufgaben

  • Product Management: Recherche von Inhaltsstoffen, Inhaltsstoff-Checks, Abstimmung mit Herstellern und Begleitung von Produkt-Tests für neue Produktlinien.
  • Brand: Unterstützung bei der Weiterentwicklung unserer visuellen Identität, unseres Verpackungsdesigns und unserer Markenbotschaften.
  • Marketing: Aktive Unterstützung im Tagesgeschäft verschiedener Marketingkanäle (CRM, Social Media, Content-Strategien & Community-Feedback).
  • Market & Competitor Intelligence: Beobachtung von Trends im Bereich Männerpflege & Wellbeing sowie Erstellung von Wettbewerbsanalysen.
  • Allround-Hands-on: Flexible Unterstützung unseres Teams überall dort, wo im E-Commerce-Alltag gerade Support benötigt wird.

Qualifikation

  • Studium: Laufendes oder abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich BWL, Wirtschaftswissenschaften, Marketing, E-Commerce, Kosmetik o.ä.
  • Interesse an Produktentwicklung: Du interessierst Dich für kosmetische Formulierungen und Produktentwicklung.
  • Gespür für moderne Marken: Du verstehst, wie moderne Marken kommunizieren, und hast Lust, Content-Strategien mitzudenken.
  • High Learning Agility & Smartness: Du erfasst komplexe Themen blitzschnell und hast keine Scheu vor Aufgaben, die Du noch nie gemacht hast.
  • Digital-DNA & Tool-Skill: Du nutzt moderne Tools und KI ganz selbstverständlich, um Deine Arbeit effizienter zu machen.
  • Struktur & Belastbarkeit: Du behältst auch bei verschiedenen parallelen Aufgaben den Überblick und kannst nach Prioritäten filtern.
  • Macher-Mentalität: Du wartest nicht auf Anweisungen, sondern bringst eigene Vorschläge mit.

Du passt perfekt zu uns, wenn Du …

  • Anpacken willst, statt nur Theorie aus der Uni zu wiederholen.
  • Lust auf die Kombination aus Produktentwicklung, Markenstrategie und operativem Marketing-Support hast.
  • Neue Tools, Marketing-Trends und Methoden extrem schnell aufsaugst und ausprobierst.
  • Eine hohe Eigenverantwortung schätzt und gerne aktiv mitgestaltest.
  • Energie, Humor und einen positiven Vibe ins Team bringst.

Wir sind kein Match, wenn …

  • Du ein ruhiges Konzernpraktikum mit fest vorgeschriebenen Routineaufgaben suchst.
  • Du bei unklaren Aufgabenstellungen abwartest, bis dir jemand jeden einzelnen Schritt erklärt.
  • Du dich ausschließlich für einen isolierten Bereich interessierst (unsere Rolle verbindet Produktentwicklung, Brand & Marketingkanäle).
  • Du dich von schnellen Dynamiken leicht stressen lässt.

Benefits

  • Flexibles Arbeiten: Arbeite aus unserem Büro im Frankfurter Ostend oder hybrid.
  • Steile Lernkurve & Mentoring: Arbeite direkt mit unseren Decision-Makern im Brand- und Produktmanagement-Bereich zusammen.
  • Startup Vibes & Echte Verantwortung: Deine Ideen fließen direkt in echte Produkte, Marketing-Kampagnen und den Markenauftritt ein.
  • Benefits: Wellpass, Bikeleasing, Mitarbeiterrabatte, Corporate Benefits, Afterworks, Teamevents.
  • Feedback & Wertschätzung: Erlebe eine offene Feedback-Kultur und ehrliche Anerkennung für Deinen Einsatz.

Bereit, mit uns Störtebekker zur Men Wellbeing Brand weiterzuentwickeln? Dann bewirb Dich jetzt!

title: ITSC Manager-1st Line of Defense (m/w/d)
description:
Über uns

Bei G+D Netcetera entwickeln wir digitale Lösungen für die Finanzbranche, die dazu beitragen, Bezahlprozesse für Millionen von Menschen weltweit einfach, sicher und zuverlässig zu gestalten. Aufgaben

  • Aufbau, Einführung und Pflege des unternehmensweiten IT-Notfall- und Continuity-Managementsystems (ITSCM) einschließlich Richtlinien und Prozessen sowie Ausrichtung an Standards wie ISO 27031, ITIL, BSI 200-4 und ISO 27001.
  • Durchführung von Risiko-, Gap- und Verfügbarkeitsanalysen; Ableitung zielgerichteter Maßnahmen
  • Ableitung und Festlegung von Wiederanlaufzeiten und Kennzahlen (RTO/KPIs) auf Basis von BIA-Ergebnissen und SLAs
  • Erstellung technischer ITSCM-Konzepte und IT-Recovery Pläne
  • Planung, Durchführung und Auswertung regelmäßiger ITSC-Tests/Übungen sowie Einleitung von Verbesserungsmaßnahmen.
  • Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Bereichen wie Business Continuity Management (BCM), Informationssicherheitsmanagement (ISM), Krisenmanagement und Cyber Incident Response sowie Unterstützung bei Schulungen und Sensibilisierung der Fachbereiche

Qualifikation

  • Abgeschlossenes Studium in Informatik, Wirtschaftsinformatik, IT-Management oder einem vergleichbaren Studiengang
  • Mehrjährige Erfahrung in IT-Service-Management und/oder IT-Notfall-/Continuity-Management sowie Projektmanagement
  • Fundiertes Verständnis von IT-Infrastrukturen, Risiken, Notfallprozessen und Recovery-Strategien
  • Erfahrung mit Normen und Standards wie ISO 27031, ITIL, ISO 22301, ISO 27001, KritisV, DORA
  • Starke kommunikative Fähigkeiten und Teamfähigkeit sowie analytisches Denken, eine strukturierte Arbeitsweise und proaktives Prozessdenken
  • Sehr gute Deutsch- und gute Englischkenntnisse
